name: Rob Portman
subject: The general election has started
body:
| Peter -- The results are in. With your help, tonight I won the Republican primary and we’re headed to the general election against Hillary Clinton’s hand-picked candidate, Ted Strickland. I could not have done this without you! However, the stakes just got higher. National Democrats have said that the path to winning both the White House and the Senate Majority runs right through Ohio. Ted Strickland is using Hillary Clinton’s campaign machine and millions of dollars of outside money to try to defeat us in November. We can’t let Ted Strickland win. It is time to show Ohio and the National Democrats that we are united to defeat Ted Strickland in November. Tomorrow, I am announcing my General Election Victory Fund push, but I am asking for your help tonight. I am hoping that you will be one of my first donors to the General Election Victory Fund. Please help me send a message to Ted Strickland by donating here: [robportman.com/general_election_victory_fund
](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/general_election_victory_fund?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gen_election_01&n=1)[
](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/general_election_victory_fund?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gen_election_01&n=2) [](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/general_election_victory_fund?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gen_election_01&n=3)
[](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/general_election_victory_fund?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gen_election_01&n=4)
[
](https://robportman.nationbuilder.com/general_election_victory_fund?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=gen_election_01&n=5)In this election, Ohio voters will have a clear choice between moving forward with our pro-growth policies for higher wages and more jobs or a return to former Governor Ted Strickland’s policies of higher taxes, burdensome regulations and bigger government.

We’re working hard already – I am traveling all over the state meeting with Ohioans about the campaign and my vision for the future. We’ve contacted over 1.3 million voters, have more than 250 volunteer county chairs across Ohio's 88 counties, and we've garnered over 15,000 endorsements from grassroots activists across Ohio. Thank you for all your support, Peter. Rob

name: Rand Paul
subject: She let it slip
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate
body:
Dear Patriot,

Whenever you hear Hillary Clinton speak the
truth, it's usually by accident.
I suspect that's what happened Sunday when
she told a crowd of supporters, "We're going to put a lot of coal
miners and coal companies out of business."

Patriot, shouldn't our elected officials be
spending more time trying to put people back to work instead of
figuring out new ways to destroy jobs?

I think so.

And if you agree, I must be able to count on
your most generous contribution of $50, $25 or even $10 right
away.

Will you sign my
petition to support coal and save jobs?

Coal has long been critical to Kentucky's
economy.

From power generation, to steel production,
to water purification, to carbon fiber and silicon metal, coal's
uses are almost too many to list.

Yet, this industry is being continually
targeted by radical environmentalists and their pals in the
Democrat Party who are now openly admitting their goal is to use
the power of government to put people out of work!

Patriot, the more restrictions we put on
American energy production -- including coal -- the more we're
hamstringing our fragile economy, the more dependent we become on
foreign sources of energy and the harder it's going to be to dig
ourselves out of $19 TRILLION in debt.

Every day in the U.S. Senate, I'm fighting to
lift outrageous restrictions on American energy production --
including Kentucky coal.

I believe, if we are going to turn our
country around -- and restore freedom and economic prosperity in
America -- coal and the wonderful men and women who work in this
industry are going to play a vital role.

I'm fighting hard for them every day in the
U.S. Senate.

I'm asking you to stand with me by making
your most generous contribution of $50, $25 or even $10 right
away.

In liberty,
Rand Paul

name: Rand Paul
subject: One heck of a fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate
body:
Dear Patriot,
President Obama thinks he can propose a new
nominee to the Supreme Court and force through in his last few
months in office. But let's face it. This is a a HUGE conflict of
interest.
Maybe the biggest of his presidency.
Many of the president's own executive actions
on immigration, climate change, and Obamacare, are under review
by the Supreme Court right now.
He has every motivation to nominate a
sympathetic judge. But just because the president gets to
nominate, doesn't mean the Senate must confirm.
And let me tell you, I am not simply going to
let him have his way without a heck of a fight. I'm going to do
everything I can to make sure the Senate does what is necessary
to uphold the Constitution.
Patriot, President Obama thinks he should
have the power to create immigration law out nothing.
He thinks he has the power to basically
cripple entire industries like coal without ever having been
given that power by Congress.
It's a complete Constitutional debate on
whose powers it is to legislate right now.
Is it the president or Congress?
Obama's entire philosophy of law is up for
debate and it will be decided where? At the Supreme Court.
And we know EXACTLY how he will want his
nominee to rule. It's easy to see why he's so eager to fill the
seat before Americans have their say in November.
The president needs a sympathetic character.
He needs a nominee who supports the notion that the executive
branch should be more powerful than the legislative -- that the
president is the ultimate authority and that Congress, the voice
of the people, doesn't matter at all.
I will not let this happen.
I'm not going to take it lying down and let
the president have his way.
Will you stand with me as I do everything I
can to block President Obama's attempt to silence the opinion of
the American public?

In Liberty,
Rand Paul

But just because the president gets to nominate, doesn't mean the Senate must confirm. And let me tell you, I am not simply going to let him have his way without a heck of a fight. I'm going to do everything I can to make sure the Senate does what is necessary to uphold the Constitution. Patriot, President Obama thinks he should have the power to create immigration law out nothing. He thinks he has the power to basically cripple entire industries like coal without ever having been given that power by Congress. It's a complete Constitutional debate on whose powers it is to legislate right now. Is it the president or Congress? Obama's entire philosophy of law is up for debate and it will be decided where? At the Supreme Court. And we know EXACTLY how he will want his nominee to rule. It's easy to see why he's so eager to fill the seat before Americans have their say in November. The president needs a sympathetic character. He needs a nominee who supports the notion that the executive branch should be more powerful than the legislative -- that the president is the ultimate authority and that Congress, the voice of the people, doesn't matter at all. I will not let this happen. I'm not going to take it lying down and let the president have his way. Will you stand with me as I do everything I can to block President Obama's attempt to silence the opinion of the American public? | Peter \-- Today marks the beginning of the general election and to celebrate, we are relaunching [RetreadTed.com](http://www.retreadted.com?e=aa4131f163f0a23ba616fd24e19c0e92&utm_source=robportman&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=retreadrelaunch&n=1 "RetreadTed.com") with a new look and a new web video detailing Governor Strickland's "Mixed and Spotty" record!

name: Russ Feingold
subject: Judge Merrick Garland deserves fair consideration
disclaimer_text: paid for by Russ for Wisconsin, Joe Sensenbrenner, Madison, WI, Treasurer
body:
It's officially time for the Senate to do its job. | [](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41da/1c469400/5a3d9ff/3519255/426561571/VEsH/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/SCOTUSnom?utm_source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_campaign=20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_medium=ema")
## BREAKING NEWS:
Friend, President Obama just announced his nominee for the next justice of the Supreme Court: Judge Merrick Garland. **[Now, it's the Senate's turn to do its job. Judge Garland is a highly credentialed jurist, and he deserves a full and fair hearing by the Senate Judiciary Committee. Add your name if you agree.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41da/1c469400/5a3d9ff/3519255/426561571/VEsE/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/SCOTUSnom?utm_source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_campaign=20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_medium=ema")** Republicans in the Senate -- Senator Ron Johnson included -- have pledged to disregard their constitutional duties by refusing to even consider the nomination. Friend, this is scary. Even former Wisconsin Governor Tommy Thompson -- a Republican -- said that the Senate should vote on a Supreme Court nominee. He's right. Partisan politics can't be an excuse to toss our Constitution out the window. When it comes to the Supreme Court, respect for the judicial branch must come before political point-scoring and campaign attacks. **[I'm sure you agree. Now, it's time for the Senate to do its job. Join me in urging Senator Johnson and his Republican colleagues in the Senate to do what's right. Judge Garland should get a fair hearing -- it's what our Constitution requires, and what our democracy deserves.](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41da/1c469400/5a3d9ff/3519255/426561571/VEsF/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/SCOTUSnom?utm_source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_campaign=20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_medium=ema")** Thanks for standing with us on this. My campaign and I will keep you updated as things evolve. Sincerely, Russ Feingold | [ADD YOUR NAME »](http://action.russfeingold.com/page/m/66cf41da/1c469400/5a3d9ff/3519255/426561571/VEsC/ "http://action.russfeingold.com/page/s/SCOTUSnom?utm_source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&source=ema_2016-03-16_20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_campaign=20160315_SCOTUS_Nominee&utm_medium=ema")

name: Marco Rubio
subject: Thank you so much
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Marco Rubio for President
body:
| | | | | | | | [](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/46649839:JdfXlNAyN:m:1:1349778030:7CBEBA598B0D5A69CD1689D3F43162EC:r)
---
| Peter, Last night in Miami, I announced I was suspending my campaign for the presidency. I'm immensely proud of what we did these last eleven months, and I'm so grateful for everything supporters like you put into it. I know it was a lot, and I appreciate every last minute and dollar. As I **[said last night](http://email.vervemail.com/ct/46649840:JdfXlNAyN:m:1:1349778030:7CBEBA598B0D5A69CD1689D3F43162EC:r)** , you are truly the best, hardest working supporters anywhere, and I tried my very best to run a campaign that was worthy of all of you. That was a campaign that was unflinchingly optimistic, and that was all about the ideals and values that have made America exceptional. Sadly, in this election, that didn't make for a winning campaign, but I hope you think it made for a worthy one -- worthy of our party, our movement, and our country. Americans are really angry, and frustrated, and I share that: they have a political establishment that doesn't fight for them, doesn't respond to their concerns, and doesn't value what they do. I know because I share those values and concerns. The answer to that will be a movement that values free enterprise, our Constitution, and our Judeo-Christian values, that doesn't look down on conservatives, that doesn't manipulate anger but tries to solve it. When that movement is revived, when we have a movement that recognizes the challenges we face but also has forward-thinking answers for them, then we will finally be on the verge of a New American Century. While this may not have been the year for a hopeful and optimistic message, I am as hopeful and optimistic about America as I have ever been. I don't want Americans to give in to anger, frustration, and hate -- we cannot afford it -- and I don't believe they well. As I said in Miami last night, I take great comfort in the ancient words that teach us that in their hearts humans plan their course, but the Lord establishes their steps. I truly mean it when I say I have the best supporters in the world. It's a great credit to you that you chose not to give in to anger and frustration, that you want America to remain not just a rich and powerful nation, but a special one. I'll never give up fighting alongside you to make that happen. Sincerely,Marco Rubio

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: How we respond to this moment
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bernie 2016
body:
When we started our campaign 10 months ago, I don't think you could find a single person who would believe you if you said Bernie Sanders would win nine states by this point in the campaign.
Last night we beat all the polls in almost every state. We earned a significant number of delegates, and are on track for the nomination. Here's why:
What you will not hear from the political and media establishment is that, **based on the primary and caucus schedule for the rest of the race, this is the high water mark for the Clinton campaign. Startingtoday, the map now shifts dramatically in our favor.**
Arizona, Idaho, and Utah are up next Tuesday. Alaska, Hawaii, and Washington State caucus the Saturday after. Then it's Wisconsin's turn to vote.
**That means we have an extremely good chance to win nearly _every state that votes in the next month._** If we continue to stand together, we're just getting started for our political revolution:
[**Make a $3 contribution to our campaign and we can win this Democratic primary, the White House, and take our country back from the billionaire class.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/what-comes-next?refcode=em160316s1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/what-comes-next?refcode=em160316s1-full")
No one said a political revolution would be easy. We are up against a billionaire class and super PACs that are determined to see us lose.
The fact remains that Hillary Clinton's lead will never be as large as it is right now. From here on out we keep chipping away until we take the lead. But that can only happen if we keep fighting, [**and that 's why your $3 contribution to our campaign is so important.**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/what-comes-next?refcode=em160316s1-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/what-comes-next?refcode=em160316s1-full")
The whole country will be watching to see how we respond in this moment. Let's send a message that millions of Americans are just as ready to fight for an economy that works for everyone as we were when this campaign started 10 months ago.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders

clean_body: When we started our campaign 10 months ago, I don't think you could find a single person who would believe you if you said Bernie Sanders would win nine states by this point in the campaign. Last night we beat all the polls in almost every state. We earned a significant number of delegates, and are on track for the nomination. Here's why: What you will not hear from the political and media establishment is that, based on the primary and caucus schedule for the rest of the race, this is the high water mark for the Clinton campaign. Startingtoday, the map now shifts dramatically in our favor. Arizona, Idaho, and Utah are up next Tuesday. Alaska, Hawaii, and Washington State caucus the Saturday after. Then it's Wisconsin's turn to vote. That means we have an extremely good chance to win nearly every state that votes in the next month. If we continue to stand together, we're just getting started for our political revolution: "" No one said a political revolution would be easy. We are up against a billionaire class and super PACs that are determined to see us lose. The fact remains that Hillary Clinton's lead will never be as large as it is right now. From here on out we keep chipping away until we take the lead. But that can only happen if we keep fighting, "" The whole country will be watching to see how we respond in this moment. Let's send a message that millions of Americans are just as ready to fight for an economy that works for everyone as we were when this campaign started 10 months ago. name: Rand Paul
subject: Very liberal view of gun rights
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rand Paul for Senate
body:
Dear Patriot,

Within minutes of President Obama's
nomination of Federal Appeals Court Judge Merrick Garland to the
U.S. Supreme Court, major concerns about his record on the Second
Amendment began to emerge.

Frankly, I'm not surprised.

But with the stakes higher than ever, I must
ask for your most generous contribution of $50, $25 or even $10
right away.

The truth is, since day one of his
presidency, President Obama has tried time and again to ram his
anti-gun agenda into law through the U.S. Congress.

When he failed, he simply resorted to
unconstitutional executive orders -- showing the lengths he'll go
to undermine our God-given right to keep and bear arms.

So I've always believed it was a forgone
conclusion President Obama would nominate someone to do his
anti-gun bidding at the Supreme Court.

Sadly, it appears I've been proven right.

As the Washington Times reported this
morning, one conservative judicial organization is describing
Judge Garland as having a "very liberal view on gun rights."

That's because, in 2007, Judge Garland was
one of four judges on a ten judge panel to rush to the aid of
Washington, D.C.'s outrageous anti-gun laws and recommend
rehearing the case that would ultimately become Heller v.
Washington, D.C.

Had Judge Garland gotten his way, the U.S.
Supreme Court may have never heard the Heller case -- meaning the
court would have never upheld the Second Amendment as an
individual right in their landmark 5-4 decision.

Now, recognizing one Justice could tip the
court's scales -- and reverse the Heller decision -- President
Obama is going all out to confirm Judge Garland.

He knows he could be just one Supreme Court
Justice away from interpreting the Second Amendment out of
existence.

Patriot, I don't know about you, but I
believe President Obama has done enough damage to America.

I believe with so much at stake, the American
people should have a say in the court's direction -- and our next
President must fill the vacancy left by Justice Scalia.

With your help, I've fought every one of
President Obama's attempts to gut our gun rights at every turn.

And I'm not about to let up now!

But I must be able to count on your support.

For my tough stance on gun rights, Democrats
are going to come after me with everything they have.

So will you please help me continue to fight
for our Second Amendment rights with your most generous
contribution of $50, $25 or even $10 right away?

In liberty,
Rand Paul

Pitch in $50 TODAY >>

Pitch in $75 TODAY >>

Pitch in $100 TODAY>>

Or donate another
amount >>

Sisters and Brothers -
For decades, Bernie Sanders has fought for us:
For health care as a right for every man, woman and child. For an economy that works for all Americans, not just a handful of wealthy elites. And to stop a slide toward oligarchy where the political life of our country is controlled by a handful of billionaire families.
And if one thing is clear after last night's results, it's that you're ready to keep fighting for him.
More than 27,315 contributions have come into our campaign since the polls closed last night, and if you add yours before midnight, we're going to send a clear message to the corporate media and political establishment that we're just getting started.
[**Can Bernie count on you to add a $3 contribution to our campaign before midnight tonight?**](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/what-comes-next?refcode=em160316s2-full "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/what-comes-next?refcode=em160316s2-full")
From here on out, the map shifts in our favor. We're going to do very well next Tuesday, and the states immediately following look just as good. If we stand together, if we keep fighting, we can win.
All my best,
Jeff Weaver

We deserve a fair and open hearing | Friend --

This morning, President Obama fulfilled his constitutional duty and nominated Judge Merrick Garland to fill the vacancy on the Supreme Court.

Immediately after, Majority Leader Mitch McConnell doubled down on his obstructionism -- he committed once again to deny Judge Garland (or any nominee) a hearing or a vote.

Let’s be clear on this: Judge Garland has a long and distinguished career on the bench and in public service, a brilliant legal mind, and a history of bipartisan support and admiration.

But Senate Republicans don't care about his qualifications. They're treating the Supreme Court vacancy as an opportunity to score political points, with complete disregard for the Constitution.

\- March 16th, 2016 -
# **So Many Great Wins Last Night!**
| Dear Peter, We had so many great wins last night! I am very proud to receive your support because without you this wouldn’t have been possible. These past few weeks have been a truly amazing experience. We met so many incredible people. I am just a messenger and it is clear that our voices are being heard! We will no longer stand for the incompetence and corrupt political system that has failed hard working American's for far too long. I am self-funding my campaign and I am going to do what is right for the people - not the special interests, donors and lobbyists - that control our all talk, no action politicians. I look forward to meeting more of you over the next few months and discussing the issues most important to voters like you including illegal immigration, tax reform, protecting your second amendment rights and national security, among others. Thank you for your continued support. Together we will Make America Great Again! Best Wishes,
**Donald J. Trump**
